coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 6–8 miles, Z2 (130–140 bpm), rolling terrain if available but nothing steep. You're in week 21 taper with 89 days to race—this is about maintaining aerobic base and legs while your CNS recovers from peak phase. Your last three runs averaged 133–142 bpm; dial it back today. Recovery score is 89/100 and resting HR stable at 61, so you have capacity, but respect the taper protocol: lower volume, lower intensity. Sunday is your second long-effort slot this week, but in taper weeks, that means easy, not structured.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 9.21h with 96.4% efficiency Friday night—excellent, but don't treat that as the norm given your travel schedule and 5.86h 30-day average. Before today's run, eat a light carb dose (banana + coffee) 30–45 min prior; post-run, hit 30–40g carbs + 15–20g protein within 30 min to begin glycogen restock. At 50K altitude and sparse aid, your gut needs to handle 60g carbs/hour during the race; easy runs are not the time to practice fueling, but maintain daily carb intake at 6–8g/kg body weight to support recovery and VO₂ adaptation.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Sleep 8–9 hours tonight; you banked it Friday, but taper is where adaptation happens in bed, not on the trails. Your 30-day sleep average of 5.86h is unsustainable and will blunt your race-day readiness—non-negotiable: prioritize 7.5–8h minimum for the next 11 weeks, especially travel nights. Use foam roll / lacrosse ball on calves and feet (60 sec each leg) post-run and before bed. No sauna, no ice bath; active recovery only (easy walking, light mobility).

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 89 days from a 2,500m vert alpine race with sparse aid and technical ground—that demands respect and specificity, not fatigue. Taper anxiety is real; resisting the urge to add a hard session or extra miles now is the hardest mental work you'll do. Review your race plan this week: aid station fueling strategy, expected time cutoffs per section, altitude acclimatization (when do you arrive in Kranjska Gora?), and shoe/gear logistics. Mental edge in taper is logistics clarity and sleep debt paydown, not more grit.
